en: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for the miseries that are coming upon you.
zh: 你們富有的人哪，應當為那將要臨到你們的災禍哭泣哀號。

en: Your riches have rotted and your garments are moth-eaten.
zh: 你們的財物朽壞了，你們的衣服給蛀了，

en: Your gold and silver have corroded, and their corrosion will be evidence against you and will eat your flesh like fire. You have laid up treasure in the last days.
zh: 你們的金銀生鏽，這鏽要成為控告你們的鐵證，又要像火一樣吞吃你們的肉。你們竟然在這末後的日子積聚財寶。

en: Behold, the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you kept back by fraud, are crying out against you, and the cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of hosts.
zh: 看哪，工人為你們收割莊稼，你們竟然剋扣他們的工資；那工資必為他們呼冤；收割者的呼聲，已經達到萬軍之主的耳中了。

en: You have lived on the earth in luxury and in self-indulgence. You have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
zh: 你們在世上窮奢極侈，養肥了自己，竟不知屠宰的日子到了。

en: You have condemned; you have murdered the righteous person. He does not resist you.
zh: 你們把義人定罪殺害，但他並沒有反抗。

en: Be patient, therefore, brothers, until the coming of the Lord. See how the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, being patient about it, until it receives the early and the late rains.
zh: 所以，弟兄們，你們應當忍耐，直到主來。看哪，農夫等待著地裡寶貴的出產，為它忍耐，直到獲得秋霖春雨。

en: You also, be patient. Establish your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is at hand.
zh: 你們也應當忍耐，堅定自己的心；因為主再來的日子近了。

en: Do not grumble against one another, brothers, so that you may not be judged; behold, the Judge is standing at the door.
zh: 弟兄們，不要彼此抱怨，免得你們受審判。看哪，審判的主已經站在門前了。

en: As an example of suffering and patience, brothers, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
zh: 弟兄們，你們應當效法奉主的名說話的先知，以他們為受苦忍耐的榜樣。

en: Behold, we consider those blessed who remained steadfast. You have heard of the steadfastness of Job, and you have seen the purpose of the Lord, how the Lord is compassionate and merciful.
zh: 看哪，那些忍耐的人，我們稱他們是有福的；你們聽過約伯的忍耐，也看見了主賜給他的結局，知道主是滿有憐憫和仁慈的。

en: But above all, my brothers, do not swear, either by heaven or by earth or by any other oath, but let your "yes" be yes and your "no" be no, so that you may not fall under condemnation.
zh: 我的弟兄們，最要緊的是不可起誓：不可指著天起誓，也不可指著地起誓，任何的誓都當禁絕。你們的話，是就說是，不是就說不是；免得你們落在審判之下。

en: Is anyone among you suffering? Let him pray. Is anyone cheerful? Let him sing praise.
zh: 你們中間有人受苦嗎？他就應該禱告。有人心情愉快嗎？他就應該歌頌。

en: Is anyone among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the church, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord.
zh: 你們中間有人患病嗎？他就應該請教會的長老來，讓他們奉主的名為他抹油祈禱。

en: And the prayer of faith will save the one who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up. And if he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.
zh: 出於信心的祈禱，可以使病人康復，主必叫他起來；他若犯了罪，也必蒙赦免。

en: Therefore, confess your sins to one another and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person has great power as it is working.
zh: 所以你們應當彼此認罪，互相代求，這樣你們就可以痊愈。義人祈禱所發出的力量，是大有功效的。

en: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed fervently that it might not rain, and for three years and six months it did not rain on the earth.
zh: 以利亞是與我們性情相同的人；他懇切祈求不要下雨，地上就三年零六個月沒有雨；

en: Then he prayed again, and heaven gave rain, and the earth bore its fruit.
zh: 他又祈禱，天就下雨，地就生出土產來。

en: My brothers, if anyone among you wanders from the truth and someone brings him back,
zh: 我的弟兄們，你們中間若有人被騙離開了真道，如果有人使他回頭，

en: let him know that whoever brings back a sinner from his wandering will save his soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.
zh: 你們應該知道，那使罪人從歧途上轉回的，就會拯救他的靈魂脫離死亡，也會遮蓋許多罪惡。
